revision.text: #REDIRECT[[従六位#正七位]]

revision.comment: 「従六位」から別項化しました
revision.text:
'''正七位'''（しょうしちい）は、[[日本]]の[[位階]]における位の一つ。[[従六位]]の下、[[従七位]]の上に位する。
[[大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）、他の位階とともに[[大宝律令]]において初めて制定された。律令制において正七位は、さらに正七位上と正七位下の二階に分けられた。[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官制]]においては、上下の別がなくされ、[[神祇官]]の権大史、[[太政官]]の権少史などの官職に相当する位階とされた。
[[昭和]]21年（[[1946年]]）の生存者叙位停止まで、正七位は、文官では高等官6等（[[警視]]など）、武官では大尉（[[中隊長]]など）の初叙位階とされた。停止後は、故人のみを対象として、省庁の地方出先機関の課長級職員、警視の階級にあった[[警察官]]などが叙位されている。
著名な正七位の叙位の例としては、[[十和田湖]]の開発に尽力した[[和井内貞行]]、[[ときわ台駅 (東京都)#宮本警部の殉職|東武東上線ときわ台駅で殉職した警視庁警部の宮本邦彦]]の叙位などがある。
== 外部リンク == * [http://law.e-gov.go.jp/htmldata/T15/T15CO325.html 位階令] {{ja icon}} - 法令データ提供システム * [http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/BIImgFrame.php?JP_NUM=40022968&VOL_NUM=00004&KOMA=168&ITYPE=0 官位相当表] {{ja icon}} - 国立国会図書館近代デジタルライブラリー
{{日本の位階}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆうしちい}} [[Category:日本の位階|しゆうしちい]]

[[昭和]]21年（[[1946年]]）の生存者叙位停止まで、正七位は、[[文官]]では[[高等官]]6等（[[警視]]など）、[[武官]]では[[大尉]]（[[中隊長]]など）の初叙位階とされた。停止後は、故人のみを対象として、省庁の地方出先機関の課長級職員、警視の階級にあった[[日本の警察官|警察官]]などが叙位されている。
著名な正七位の叙位の例としては、[[十和田湖]]の開発に尽力した[[和井内貞行]]、[[ときわ台駅 (東京都)#宮本警部の殉職|東武東上線ときわ台駅で殉職した警視庁警部の宮本邦彦]]の叙位などがある。

{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}} '''正七位'''（しょうしちい）は、[[日本]]の[[位階]]における位の一つ。[[従六位]]の下、[[従七位]]の上に位する。
== 解説 == [[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）、他の位階とともに[[大宝律令]]において初めて制定された。律令制において正七位は、さらに正七位上と正七位下の二階に分けられた。[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官制]]においては、上下の別がなくされ、[[神祇官]]の権大史、[[太政官]]の権少史などの官職に相当する位階とされた。
[[昭和]]21年（[[1946年]]）の生存者叙位停止まで、正七位は、[[文官]]では[[高等官]]6等（[[警視]]など）、[[武官]]では[[大尉]]（[[中隊長]]など）の初叙位階とされた。停止後は、故人のみを対象として、省庁の地方出先機関の課長級職員、警視の階級にあった[[日本の警察官|警察官]]などが叙位されている。
著名な正七位の叙位の例としては、[[十和田湖]]の開発に尽力した[[和井内貞行]]、[[ときわ台駅 (東京都)#宮本警部の殉職|東武東上線ときわ台駅で殉職した警視庁警部の宮本邦彦]]の叙位などがある。

It is positioned below {{ill|Shichii|ja|従六位}} and above {{ill|Jūshichii|ja|従七位}}. == Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Shichii|ja|従六位}} and above {{ill|Jūshichii|ja|従七位}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Sadafuku Wainai|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive {{日本の位階}}
[[Category:Rank system of Japan]]
[[ja:正七位]]

== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Sadafuku Wainai|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Ranks of Japan]] to [[Category:Original page]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Shichii|ja|従六位}} and above {{ill|Jūshichii|ja|従七位}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Sadafuku Wainai|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

revision.comment: 外部リンクの修正 (laws.e-gov.go.jp) ([[Wikipedia:Bot|Bot]]による編集)
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}} '''正七位'''（しょうしちい）は、[[日本]]の[[位階]]における位の一つ。[[従六位]]の下、[[従七位]]の上に位する。
== 解説 == [[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）、他の位階とともに[[大宝律令]]において初めて制定された。律令制において正七位は、さらに正七位上と正七位下の二階に分けられた。[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官 (明治時代)|太政官制]]においては、上下の別がなくされ、[[神祇官 (明治時代)|神祇官]]の権大史、[[太政官 (明治時代)|太政官]]の権少史などの官職に相当する位階とされた。
[[昭和]]21年（[[1946年]]）の生存者叙位停止まで、正七位は、[[文官]]では[[高等官]]6等（[[警視]]など）、[[武官]]では[[大尉]]（[[中隊長]]など）の初叙位階とされた。停止後は、故人のみを対象として、省庁の地方出先機関の課長級職員、警視の階級にあった[[日本の警察官|警察官]]などが叙位されている。
著名な正七位の叙位の例としては、[[十和田湖]]の開発に尽力した[[和井内貞行]]、東武東上線[[ときわ台駅 (東京都)|ときわ台駅]]で殉職した警視庁警部の[[宮本邦彦 (警察官)|宮本邦彦]]の叙位などがある。
== 外部リンク == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/215IO0000000325 位階令] - [[e-Gov法令検索]] * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - 国立国会図書館近代デジタルライブラリー
{{日本の位階}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆうしちい}} [[Category:位階制度|しゆうしちい]]

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Sadafuku Wainai|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Sadafuku Wainai|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.

'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.

'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "正五位}}" to "正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank}}"
revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

revision.text:
[[Category:new pages]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

[[Category:new pages]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}.

'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官 (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.

revision.text:
[[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

revision.text:
[[Category:Completion tier 3]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}.
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

[[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] {{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, along with other ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subranks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}} abolished these subranks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)}}.
Until the suspension of conferring ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官}} at the rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive
<!--Senior Seventh Rank--><!--Senior Seventh Rank-->{{Court Ranks of Japan}} * <!--ranklist: {{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank}} {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}} {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank}} {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}} {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank}} {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank}} {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位|1=Senior Sixth Rank}} {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}} {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} {{ill|Senior Eighth Rank|ja|正八位|1=Senior Eighth Rank}} {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} {{ill|Senior Ninth Rank|ja|正九位|1=Senior Ninth Rank}} {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}} {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank}} {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank}} endranklist--><!--Senior Seventh Rank-->

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015年8月}} '''正七位'''（しょうしちい）は、[[日本]]の[[位階]]における位の一つ。[[従六位]]の下、[[従七位]]の上に位する。
== 解説 == [[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）、他の位階とともに[[大宝律令]]において初めて制定された。律令制において正七位は、さらに正七位上と正七位下の二階に分けられた。[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官 (明治時代)|太政官制]]においては、上下の別がなくされ、[[神祇官 (明治時代)|神祇官]]の権大史、[[太政官 (明治時代)|太政官]]の権少史などの官職に相当する位階とされた。
[[昭和]]21年（[[1946年]]）の生存者叙位停止まで、正七位は、[[文官]]では[[高等官]]6等（[[警視]]など）、[[武官]]では[[大尉]]（[[中隊長]]など）の初叙位階とされた。停止後は、故人のみを対象として、省庁の地方出先機関の課長級職員、警視の階級にあった[[日本の警察官|警察官]]などが叙位されている。
著名な正七位の叙位の例としては、[[十和田湖]]の開発に尽力した[[和井内貞行]]、東武東上線[[ときわ台駅 (東京都)|ときわ台駅]]で殉職した警視庁警部の[[宮本邦彦 (警察官)|宮本邦彦]]の叙位などがある。
== 外部リンク == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/215IO0000000325 位階令] - [[e-Gov法令検索]] * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - 国立国会図書館近代デジタルライブラリー
{{日本の位階}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆうしちい}} [[Category:位階制度|しゆうしちい]]

著名な正七位の叙位の例としては、[[十和田湖]]の開発に尽力した[[和井内貞行]]、東武東上線[[ときわ台駅 (東京都)|ときわ台駅]]で殉職した警視庁警部の[[宮本邦彦 (警察官)|宮本邦彦]]の叙位などがある。
== 外部リンク == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/215IO0000000325 位階令] - [[e-Gov法令検索]] * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - 国立国会図書館近代デジタルライブラリー

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taihō|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with other Ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|qq=draft|qq=draft}} abolished these subRanks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Until the suspension of conferring Ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial Rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the Rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector Rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|ja|和井内貞行|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|ja|十和田湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|ja|ときわ台駅 (東京都)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

{{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}

'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==
Shōshichii was first established in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō]]元年 (701) as part of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], along with other Ranks. Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōshichii was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} abolished these subRanks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|qq=|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.

Until the suspension of conferring Ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial Rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil officials|qq=|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōkan|qq=}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military officers|qq=|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} at the Rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector Rank, and similar positions.

Notable recipients of Shōshichii include [[:en:Wainai Sadayuki|Wainai Sadayuki]], who contributed to the development of [[:en:Lake Towada|Lake Towada]], and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Miyamoto|qq=}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at [[:en:Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)]] on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.

== External links ==

[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}

'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==
Shōshichii was first established in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō]]元年 (701) as part of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], along with other Ranks. Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōshichii was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} abolished these subRanks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.

Until the suspension of conferring Ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial Rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil officials|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōkan|qq=}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military officers|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} at the Rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector Rank, and similar positions.

Notable recipients of Shōshichii include [[:en:Wainai Sadayuki|Wainai Sadayuki]], who contributed to the development of [[:en:Lake Towada|Lake Towada]], and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Miyamoto|qq=}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at [[:en:Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)]] on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正七位]][[ko:정7위]][[zh:正七位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545345]] Wikidata name: Senior Seventh Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō]]元年 (701) as part of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], along with other Ranks. Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōshichii was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} abolished these subRanks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Until the suspension of conferring Ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial Rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil officials|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōkan|qq=}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military officers|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} at the Rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector Rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include [[:en:Wainai Sadayuki|Wainai Sadayuki]], who contributed to the development of [[:en:Lake Towada|Lake Towada]], and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Miyamoto|qq=}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at [[:en:Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)]] on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正七位]][[ko:정7위]][[zh:正七位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545345]] Wikidata name: Senior Seventh Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō]]元年 (701) as part of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], along with other Ranks. Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōshichii was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} abolished these subRanks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Until the suspension of conferring Ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial Rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil officials|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōkan|qq=}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military officers|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} at the Rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector Rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include [[:en:Wainai Sadayuki|Wainai Sadayuki]], who contributed to the development of [[:en:Lake Towada|Lake Towada]], and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Miyamoto|qq=}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at [[:en:Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)]] on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正七位]][[ko:정7위]][[zh:正七位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545345]] Wikidata name: Senior Seventh Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō]]元年 (701) as part of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], along with other Ranks. Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōshichii was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} abolished these subRanks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Until the suspension of conferring Ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial Rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil officials|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōkan|qq=}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military officers|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} at the Rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector Rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include [[:en:Wainai Sadayuki|Wainai Sadayuki]], who contributed to the development of [[:en:Lake Towada|Lake Towada]], and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Miyamoto|qq=}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at [[:en:Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)]] on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.

'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō]]元年 (701) as part of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], along with other Ranks. Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], Shōshichii was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} abolished these subRanks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Until the suspension of conferring Ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial Rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil officials|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōkan|qq=}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military officers|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} at the Rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector Rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include [[:en:Wainai Sadayuki|Wainai Sadayuki]], who contributed to the development of [[:en:Lake Towada|Lake Towada]], and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Miyamoto|qq=}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at [[:en:Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)]] on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive

'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}}, along with other Ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} abolished these subRanks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Until the suspension of conferring Ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial Rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil officials|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōkan|qq=}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military officers|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} at the Rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector Rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|en|Wainai Sadayuki|WD=Q7960142|lt=Wainai Sadayuki}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|en|Lake Towada|WD=Q1196132|lt=Lake Towada}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Miyamoto|qq=}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|en|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q7813626|lt=Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
== External links ==

'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}}, along with other Ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} abolished these subRanks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Until the suspension of conferring Ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial Rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil officials|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōkan|qq=}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military officers|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} at the Rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector Rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|en|Wainai Sadayuki|WD=Q7960142|lt=Wainai Sadayuki}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|en|Lake Towada|WD=Q1196132|lt=Lake Towada}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Miyamoto|qq=}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|en|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q7813626|lt=Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.

'''Shōshichii''' (正七位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == Shōshichii was first established in {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō|1=Taiho}}元年 (701) as part of the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}, along with other Ranks. Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, Shōshichii was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Shōshichii (正七位上) and Lower Shōshichii (正七位下). During the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} abolished these subRanks, and Shōshichii was equivalent to positions such as Assistant Grand Recorder (権大史) in the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} and Assistant Junior Recorder (権少史) in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}.
Until the suspension of conferring Ranks to living persons in 1946 (Shōwa 21), Shōshichii was the initial Rank for {{ill|Civil officials|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil officials|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} at the {{ill|Kōtōkan|ja|高等官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtōkan|qq=}} 6th grade (e.g., police inspectors) and {{ill|Military officers|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military officers|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} at the Rank of captain (e.g., company commanders). After the suspension, Shōshichii was awarded posthumously, typically to local branch directors of government ministries, police officers at the inspector Rank, and similar positions.
Notable recipients of Shōshichii include {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|en|Wainai Sadayuki|WD=Q7960142|lt=Wainai Sadayuki|1=Wainai Sadayuki}}, who contributed to the development of {{ill|Lake Towada|en|Lake Towada|WD=Q1196132|lt=Lake Towada|1=Lake Towada}}, and {{ill|Kunihiko Miyamoto|ja|宮本邦彦 (警察官)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Miyamoto|qq=}}, a Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department inspector who died in the line of duty at {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|en|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q7813626|lt=Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|1=Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)}} on the Tōbu Tōjō Line.
